skill.name: ml-pipeline-workflow
skill.description: Guides users through designing end-to-end MLOps pipelines, including data preparation, training, validation, deployment, monitoring, and rollback planning.
skill.summary: Plan production ML pipelines from data preparation through deployment and monitoring.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks and weak cryptography patterns, but the flagged lines are Markdown prose, inline file references, and fenced examples. No executable scripts, network access, credential handling, prompt injection text, or cryptographic API usage were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby/shell backtick execution findings are Markdown inline code references and code fence markers. No command execution API or runnable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain Markdown backticks or fenced examples, not Ruby or shell execution syntax. No surrounding text asks the agent to execute those snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings occur in ML pipeline prose and headings. No hash function, cipher, checksum, or cryptographic library call is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are descriptive text such as the skill description, overview, and section headings. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: ML teams need repeatable pipelines that connect data, training, validation, deployment, and monitoring. This skill provides structured guidance for planning MLOps workflows and operational checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains core stages for end-to-end ML pipeline design.
Outlines data preparation, validation, and feature engineering concerns.
Describes training workflow practices, experiment tracking, and model registries.
Summarizes deployment strategies such as canary, blue-green, and rollback planning.
Lists orchestration and serving tools commonly used in production MLOps.
limitations: It is guidance-only and does not include executable automation.
Referenced resources and asset files are not present in this package.
It does not generate complete infrastructure configurations by itself.
Users must adapt recommendations to their data, model, cloud, and compliance needs.

use_cases.title: Design a New MLOps Workflow
use_cases.description: Create a stage-by-stage plan for data ingestion, training, validation, deployment, and monitoring.

use_cases.title: Review Pipeline Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether an existing pipeline includes validation, rollback, observability, and versioning practices.

use_cases.title: Compare Orchestration Options
use_cases.description: Map pipeline requirements to tools such as Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow Pipelines, or Prefect.

prompt_templates.title: Sketch a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a simple model workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ml-pipeline-workflow skill to outline a basic ML pipeline for my project. Include each stage and its main purpose.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ml-pipeline-workflow skill to improve my ML pipeline plan with data checks, model validation, monitoring, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Orchestration Approach
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ml-pipeline-workflow skill to compare Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow Pipelines, and Prefect for my ML workflow requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Deployment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release planning for production models.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ml-pipeline-workflow skill to design a production deployment workflow with canary rollout, baseline comparison, alerts, and rollback steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a fraud detection training pipeline.
output: A staged workflow covering ingestion, validation, feature generation, training, evaluation, registry approval, canary release, and monitoring.
A checklist for drift checks, baseline comparison, rollback triggers, and owner handoffs.
A gap analysis against deployment practices such as shadow testing, canary releases, automated rollback, and latency monitoring.
A prioritized action list for production readiness.
A comparison of orchestration options based on team skills, Kubernetes needs, data assets, scheduling, and observability.
A recommended shortlist with tradeoffs and next validation steps.

best_practices: Define pipeline stages, ownership, inputs, outputs, and validation checks before selecting tools.
Track data versions, model artifacts, experiment metrics, and deployment approvals together.
Start with a simple pipeline and add monitoring, canaries, and retraining triggers as risk grows.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy models without baseline comparison, rollback criteria, or production monitoring.
Do not mix training and serving concerns without clear artifact boundaries.
Do not treat orchestration tools as a substitute for data quality and model validation.

faq.question: Does this skill run ML training jobs?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning guidance and workflow structure, but it does not execute jobs.

faq.question: Which orchestration tools does it discuss?
faq.answer: It mentions Airflow, Dagster, Kubeflow Pipelines, and Prefect as common workflow options.

faq.question: Can it help with model deployment strategy?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers canary releases, blue-green deployments, shadow testing, rollback planning, and monitoring concerns.

faq.question: Does it include ready-to-use templates?
faq.answer: The skill text references templates, but this package only contains SKILL.md in the audited file structure.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated environments?
faq.answer: It can support planning, but users must add required governance, privacy, security, and audit controls.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
